title: A block whose array key holds a non-array value takes the no-items resync path and migrates without validation
description: A block file whose declared array key holds a non-array value (e.g. tasks: {}) is treated as having no items by the resync hasItems probe, so a catalog-ahead version bump resyncs it as migrated without validating the malformed content against the new schema — it never blocks, never marks. Uninvestigated — root cause confirmation, the class across the resync/validate surface, and prior-art are for the investigating agent.

evidence.reference: the resyncSchema hasItems probe (forEachBlockArray over the parsed block) yields no items for a non-array value at the array key; the no-items arm returns migrated without running validate

impact: A malformed block silently passes a version bump unvalidated; the blocked/marker diagnostic surface never engages for it.
proposed_resolution: To be determined by the investigation (Experience-Gap Handling): whether the no-items arm must distinguish absent/empty arrays from malformed non-array content, and the class across every consumer of the hasItems probe.
